commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Forecast items lack a container or visual separation, making them look cramped when the component is narrow.
issues.fix: Wrap forecast items in a small card or background element to define the strip area more clearly.

issues.description: Component is purely static; no hover effects or subtle interaction states for the forecast items.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover background or scale effect to the forecast items to indicate they are data-driven tiles.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, uses the design system appropriately, and handles all requested props efficiently. While the layout is clean, it could benefit from improved visual hierarchy for the forecast strip to ensure it doesn't look like a floating list.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid responsive layout with good spacing and proper use of Stack/Divider primitives. However, it falls short on design tokens by including hardcoded fallback colors (hex values like #18181b) instead of relying purely on CSS variables, and lacks interactive polish with no hover/focus states or transitions on the forecast items.
